is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum drug resistance markers Pfcrt T76 and Pfmdr-1 Y86.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfcrt codon 76 (T76) and Pfmdr1 codon 86 (Y86), both curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives % (n/N) for Pfcrt K76/T76 and Pfmdr-1 N86/Y86 across TTF, new infection, and ACPR groups (e.g. ACPR mutant T76 59.2% (100/169)).

sub_country_location.evidence: Study conducted in two sites (north EIR 15-20, centre EIR 50-60), but Table 1 pools frequencies by treatment outcome only. It states prevalence 'did not differ between sites' but no per-site numerator/denominator breakdown is provided.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No collection year or window is stated anywhere in the paper; cannot bind the frequencies to a <=3 year interval.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates collected from children aged 6 months to 15 years in Burkina Faso via WHO 28-day in vivo test.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary in vivo efficacy trial with molecular marker analysis (short communication reporting original data).

duplicate_risk.evidence: Routine field collection in Burkina Faso; no mention of consortium or shared dataset, though authors reference related prior Burkina Faso studies by same group.

supplement.evidence: Per-site frequency breakdown (north vs centre) would lift this to sub-country resolution but is not tabulated; such data, plus collection dates, may reside in supplementary material not provided.

assessment.summary: Primary in vivo amodiaquine efficacy study in Burkina Faso reporting extractable Pfcrt T76 and Pfmdr-1 Y86 frequencies (Table 1), but frequencies are pooled by treatment outcome across two sites without per-site breakdown, and no collection year/window is stated, failing sub-country spatial and temporal resolution requirements.
exclusion_reasons: Frequencies pooled across two sites by treatment outcome with no per-site (sub-country) numerator/denominator breakdown
No collection date or year given, so frequencies cannot be bound to a <=3-year window
